mission.title: Install and Configure RabbitMQ Cluster Operator
mission.description: The RabbitMQ Cluster Operator is a Kubernetes operator designed to deploy and manage RabbitMQ clusters efficiently. It automates the lifecycle management of RabbitMQ clusters, including creation, upgrades, and graceful shutdowns. This operator is ideal for users looking to simplify RabbitMQ deployment in Kubernetes environments, but it requires familiarity with Kubernetes custom resources.

steps.title: Add the RabbitMQ Cluster Operator Helm repository
steps.description:
This step adds the official RabbitMQ Cluster Operator Helm repository to your Helm configuration.

```bash

helm repo add rabbitmq https://rabbitmq.github.io/cluster-operator

helm repo update

```

> This command adds the RabbitMQ operator repository.
> Updates the local Helm chart repository cache.

steps.title: Install the RabbitMQ Cluster Operator
steps.description:
This step installs the RabbitMQ Cluster Operator using Helm, which manages the deployment of RabbitMQ clusters.

```bash

helm install rabbitmq-operator rabbitmq/cluster-operator --version 2.20.1 --namespace rabbitmq-system --create-namespace

```

> Installs the operator in a dedicated namespace.

steps.title: Deploy a RabbitMQ cluster
steps.description:
This step deploys a sample RabbitMQ cluster using a predefined YAML configuration.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/rabbitmq/cluster-operator/main/docs/examples/hello-world/rabbitmq.yaml

```

> Deploys a sample RabbitMQ cluster.

steps.title: Verify the RabbitMQ cluster deployment
steps.description:
This step checks the status of the RabbitMQ pods to ensure they are running correctly.

```bash

kubectl get pods -n rabbitmq-system

kubectl get rabbitmqclusters -n rabbitmq-system

```

> Lists all pods in the rabbitmq-system namespace.
> Verifies the status of the RabbitMQ cluster.

uninstall.title: Uninstall the RabbitMQ Cluster Operator
uninstall.description:
This step removes the RabbitMQ Cluster Operator and its associated resources. Be aware that this may lead to data loss if the RabbitMQ cluster is not backed up.

```bash

helm uninstall rabbitmq-operator --namespace rabbitmq-system

kubectl delete namespace rabbitmq-system

```

> Uninstalls the RabbitMQ operator.
> Deletes the namespace and all resources within it.

upgrade.title: Upgrade the RabbitMQ Cluster Operator
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you back up any important data. This step upgrades the RabbitMQ Cluster Operator to the specified version.

```bash

kubectl get pods -n rabbitmq-system -o jsonpath='{.items[*].metadata.name}' | xargs -I {} kubectl drain {} --ignore-daemonsets --delete-local-data

helm upgrade rabbitmq-operator rabbitmq/cluster-operator --version 2.20.1 --namespace rabbitmq-system

kubectl get pods -n rabbitmq-system

```

> Drains the pods to prepare for upgrade.
> Upgrades the RabbitMQ operator.
> Verifies that the pods are running after the upgrade.
commands: kubectl get pods -n rabbitmq-system -o jsonpath='{.items[*].metadata.name}' | xargs -I {} kubectl drain {} --ignore-daemonsets --delete-local-data

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to create RabbitMQ cluster'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This may occur due to insufficient permissions or misconfigured RBAC.

**Fix:**
Ensure that the service account used by the operator has the necessary permissions.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'RabbitMQ cluster is not ready'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This can happen if the RabbitMQ pods are not starting correctly due to resource constraints.

**Fix:**
Check the pod logs using 'kubectl logs <pod-name> -n rabbitmq-system' for errors.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Unable to connect to RabbitMQ'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This may indicate network issues or that the RabbitMQ service is not exposed correctly.

**Fix:**
Verify the service configuration and ensure that the correct ports are open.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'CRD not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the Custom Resource Definitions were not installed correctly.

**Fix:**
Reinstall the operator using 'kubectl apply -f https://github.com/rabbitmq/cluster-operator/releases/latest/download/cluster-operator.yml'.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for RabbitMQ 4.1.3, improved handling of cluster upgrades.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: No specific migration steps required for this version.

resolution.summary: You should see the RabbitMQ pods in the 'Running' state when executing 'kubectl get pods -n rabbitmq-system'.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a running Kubernetes cluster and the necessary tools installed.
